facts: Truck-trailer manufacturers anticipated an industry rebound in 2021 after the COVID-driven disruptions of 2020.
The economy bounced back significantly, but pandemic problems persisted regarding keeping workers on the job due to government assistance programs and finding new employees for surging demand.
Lack of steady material supply and predictable costs dragged production for many manufacturers.
Trailer manufacturers had "uncomfortable" conversations with customers regarding delivery schedules and pricing in 2021.
Nearly 270,000 units on books marked a 27% increase from the prior year.
This was the tenth consecutive year that trailer output exceeded 200,000 units.
Survey totals cannot be directly compared with domestic surveys excluding Canadian and Mexican plants; total build is larger than Top 25 numbers reported.
Survey methodology involves contacting management at each company; rankings reflect production volume, not profitability or revenue.
Hyundai Translead of San Diego, Calif., produced 55,792 truck-trailers in 2021, up 61% from 2020.
Hyundai Translead's total includes 51,101 dry freight vans; 4,308 refrigerated van trailers; and 383 flatbed trailers.
Hyundai Translead also built 11,553 container chassis and 1,497 converter dollies, not counted in rankings.
Trailers are built at Hyundai Translead's plants in Mexico.
Hyun Jin Noh, senior business strategy specialist at Hyundai Translead, reported production numbers.
Hyundai Translead added capacity to take advantage of supply partners' hard work despite 2021 supply chain challenges.
Wabash shipped 44,045 new trailers in 2021, a 21% increase compared to the prior year.
Wabash's total includes 36,640 dry freight vans; 2,875 refrigerated van trailers; 2,550 flatbeds; and 1,980 tank trailers.
Wabash also built 16,560 truck bodies and shipped 1,329 converter dollies, not counted in rankings.
President and CEO Brent Yeagy stated on a Feb 2 conference call that order backlog grew to another record level during the fourth quarter.
Yeagy credited the organization with constructing the backlog to provide options in 2022 if supply chain and workforce conditions improve.
Wabash remains focused on bringing new products and technologies to market, building out adjacent revenue streams, and growing beyond the equipment cycle.
Great Dane Limited Partnership built 38,200 trailers in 2021, up 11% over the previous year.
EVP of Sales Chris Hammond noted that supply chain and labor challenges affected the ability to fill all customer orders, pushing some into 2022.
Great Dane is optimistic that supply chains will begin to correct later in 2022, allowing growth from 2021 totals.
Utility Trailer Manufacturing Co. produced a total of 42,379 trailers, up 25% from 2020.
Utility's production includes 21,343 refrigerated trailers, 19,162 dry van trailers, and 1,874 flatbed trailers.
Marketing manager Brett Olsen cited labor market and supply chain challenges, with absenteeism from COVID variants taxing an undermanned workforce.
Utility currently has six trailer manufacturing facilities across Marion, Va.; Clearfield, Utah; Piedras Negras, Mexico; Glade Springs, Va.; Paragould, Ark.; and Enterprise, Ala.
Vanguard National Trailer Corp. produced 14,945 trailers in 2021, a 24% increase from 2020.
Vanguard's production includes 11,018 dry freight vans at its Monon, Ind. headquarters and Trenton, Georgia plant.
Vanguard built 3,297 refrigerated van trailers at its Monon plant and West Coast plant in Moreno Valley, Calif.
Stoughton Trailers produced 15,000 units in 2021, a 36% increase from the prior year.
President and CEO Bob Wahlin stated Stoughton focused on rebuilding its workforce, ramping dry van production, and installing an entirely new production line.
Stoughton purchased property for a new chassis manufacturing plant in Waco, Texas.
Stoughton installed laser cutting equipment and robotic welders to improve throughput and reduce dependency on manual labor.
MANAC Inc of Saint-Georges, Quebec, built 7,250 trailers in 2021, up 4% from 2020.
President Charles Dutil noted backlog levels would support higher output in 2022, though component availability may hold back production.
EnTrans International LLC of Athens, Tenn., reported shipments of 6,939 truck-trailers in 2021, a 15% increase from the previous year.
Senior VP Jake Radish noted all business unit backlogs are up covering well into 2023 with significant growth across segments.
EnTrans shipments include tank trailers built by Heil Trailer International of Athens, Tennessee, and Polar Tank Trailer of Holdingford, Minnesota.
EnTrans International LLC is owned by American Industrial Partners, which also owns JARCO, SERVA, and Polar Service Centers.
Fontaine Trailer Co., an Alabama-based manufacturer, manufactured 4,745 trailers in 2021, up 65% from 2020.
Commercial Platform President Alan Briley noted flatbed demand is outpacing production ability and supply chain challenges are stabilizing but not improving.
Fontaine utilizes consignment inventory programs with some supply chain partners to maintain safety stock.
Dorsey Trailer and Pitts Trailers built 4,645 trailers in 2021, a 5% increase.
President JP Pierson suggested output could have nearly doubled without supply chain and labor challenges.
Pitts Trailers implemented wage increases of 20% or more and instituted surcharges in 2021 to cover exorbitant material costs and increased logistics charges.
MAC Trailer Enterprises of Alliance, Ohio, built 6,200 trailers in 2021, a 49% increase over 2020 production.
MAC acquired Portland, Ore.-based Beall brand tankers from Wabash National at the end of 2021.
Timpte Inc in David City, Neb., built 4,037 hopper trailers in 2021, an increase of 23% from 2020.
President Dale Jones cited supplier shortages and unprecedented cost increases as challenges alongside labor availability issues.
Premier Trailer Mfg Inc in Visalia, Calif., built 2,046 trailers in 2021, a 16% decrease over the prior year.
GM Michael McGinn noted anticipation of a 20% increase in production in 2022 with initial orders for three new models including container chassis.
XPO Logistics Trailer Manufacturing in Searcy, Ark., built 2,021 units in 2021, a 14% improvement from 2020.
East Manufacturing Co of Randolph, Ohio, built 2,604 mainly aluminum trailers in 2021, up 29% from 2020.
VP Chris Cooler cited supply chain disruptions and hiring enough factory workers as the greatest challenges to increasing production capacity.
Strick Trailers built 1,800 truck-trailers in 2021, a 10% decline from the prior year.
Marketing manager Ben Katz stated the biggest headwind is difficulty getting needed parts, with daily challenges in this area.
Kentucky Trailer of Louisville, Ky., built 1,968 trailers in 2021, a decrease of 26%.
COO Gary Parker noted a significant pause in orders during April-May as Moving and Storage customers paused to see how events would develop.
Felling Trailers Inc of Sauk Centre, Minn., built 1,960 trailers with a 10K or larger axle in 2021, a 9% increase over the previous year.
IPO Fruehauf de Mexico built 3,623 truck-trailers in 2021, a 97% increase over 2020.
USA Commercial Director Don Brown anticipated a positive 2022 based on industry activity and the addition of a new facility in New Bowling Green, KY.
Fruehauf de Mexico has built trailers at its plant 20 miles north of Mexico City for over 50 years, employing some 500 workers.
Trail King Industries in Mitchell, S.D., built 1,850 truck trailers in 2021, an increase of 6% from 2020.
CFO Gene Astolfi cited supply chain issues, extended lead times, skyrocketing material costs, and labor shortages as hampering factors for 2022.
Reitnouer Inc in Birdsboro, Pa., built 2,627 trailers in 2021, a 72% increase from 2020.
President Bud Reitnouer noted constraints from labor and supply issues forced shorter-term pricing strategies due to ridiculous market conditions.
Talbert Manufacturing in Rensselear, Ind., built 1,230 trailers in 2021, a slip of 3% from 2020.
VP Troy Geisler cited supply chain interruptions, unpredictable material spikes, and limited labor pool as challenges requiring fluid planning.
Talbert added a second manufacturing plant in Liberty, North Carolina in 2014.
Western Trailer in Boise, Idaho, built 1,087 trailers in 2021, a 14% decrease over the previous year's production.
Doepker Industries in Anaheim, Sask., built 1,001 truck-trailers in 2021, down 34% from the previous year.
VP Devin Leonard noted supply chain challenges impacted output but expects increased output in 2022 while ramping up production in a newest facility in Georgetown, Ontario.
Tremcar Inc built 830 tank trailers and 260 tank trucks in 2021, with total tanks breaking even with the prior year.
Director of marketing and communications Melanie Dufresne cited parts supplier delivery delays, fluctuation of metal prices, and employee absences due to COVID-19 as challenges.
source_title: Wait 'til this year! | Trailer Body Builders
